MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Agustin R. Pina, Esq. & Reed Harvey, Esq. (415-369-9050) for Denis Francis, plaintiff and moving party / Perry Woodward, Esq. (408-299-1200) for First Citizens Bank & Trust Company, defendant and opposing party

ARGUED - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff failed to show that Defendant is acting in bad faith it attempting to foreclose on 26th Avenue property. Plaintiff failed to provide evidence to show that sale of Sloat property will yield sufficient amounts to cover both loans after tax lien is paid off. Judicially noticeable documents establish the amounts in default and contractual interest rates. Even Plaintiff's new evidence suggests that sale proceeds (available at undetermined time) will not cover the total amount owed plus taxes. See Mcenery Declaration 6. Judge Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Continued To Apr-25-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Reed E. Harvey and Agustin R. Pina (415-369-9050) appearing for Denis Francis, plaintiff / Perry Woodward (408-299-1200) appearing for First Citizens Bank, defendant

Upon commencement of the hearing at 9:40am, the Court announces its tentative ruling denying order to show cause re preliminary injunction. Matter is argued. Court rules as follows: Matter will be stayed until after the bankruptcy hearing and temporary restraining order is in effect until after the bankruptcy hearing. This matter is continued to April 25, 2013 at 9:30am. Supplemental briefs shall be filed and served by email or hand delivery by April 23, 2013 by noon. Replies shall be filed and served by email or hand delivery by April 24, 2013 by noon. Judge A. James Robertson, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
